en.content:
## Introduction

Qwen3.5 represents a significant leap forward, integrating breakthroughs in multimodal learning, architectural efficiency, reinforcement learning scale, and global accessibility to empower developers and enterprises with unprecedented capability and efficiency.

Qwen3.5-0.8B is the ultra-compact model in the Qwen3.5 family, featuring a Hybrid Attention + Mamba (Gated DeltaNet) architecture that combines full attention and linear attention layers for efficient inference. With only 0.8B parameters and ~1.73GB weight size, it is designed for edge deployment and resource-constrained scenarios, while supporting multimodal capabilities (text + image + video).

This document will show the main verification steps of the model, including supported features, feature configuration, environment preparation, single-node deployment, accuracy and performance evaluation.

The `Qwen3.5-0.8B` model is first supported in `vllm-ascend:v0.17.0rc1`.

## Supported Features

Refer to supported features to get the model's supported feature matrix.

Refer to feature guide to get the feature's configuration.

## Environment Preparation

### Model Weight

- `Qwen3.5-0.8B`(BF16 version): require 1 Atlas 800 A2 (64G × 1) NPU card. [Download model weight](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.5-0.8B) or [ModelScope](https://modelscope.cn/models/Qwen/Qwen3.5-0.8B)

It is recommended to download the model weight to the shared directory, such as `/root/.cache/` or `/tmp/models/`.

### Installation

:::::{tab-set}
::::{tab-item} Use docker image

For example, using images `quay.io/ascend/vllm-ascend:v0.17.0rc1`(for Atlas 800 A2) and `quay.io/ascend/vllm-ascend:v0.17.0rc1-a3`(for Atlas 800 A3).

Select an image based on your machine type and start the docker image on your node, refer to using docker.

```
  # Update --device according to your device (Atlas A2: /dev/davinci[0-7] Atlas A3:/dev/davinci[0-15]).
  # Update the vllm-ascend image according to your environment.
  # Note you should download the weight to /root/.cache in advance.
  # Update the vllm-ascend image
  export IMAGE=m.daocloud.io/quay.io/ascend/vllm-ascend:v0.17.0rc1
  export NAME=vllm-ascend

  # Run the container using the defined variables
  # Note: For Qwen3.5-0.8B, only 1 NPU card is needed
  docker run --rm \
  --name $NAME \
  --net=host \
  --shm-size=1g \
  --device /dev/davinci0 \
  --device /dev/davinci_manager \
  --device /dev/devmm_svm \
  --device /dev/hisi_hdc \
  -v /usr/local/dcmi:/usr/local/dcmi \
  -v /usr/local/Ascend/driver/tools/hccn_tool:/usr/local/Ascend/driver/tools/hccn_tool \
  -v /usr/local/bin/npu-smi:/usr/local/bin/npu-smi \
  -v /usr/local/Ascend/driver/lib64/:/usr/local/Ascend/driver/lib64/ \
  -v /usr/local/Ascend/driver/version.info:/usr/local/Ascend/driver/version.info \
  -v /etc/ascend_install.info:/etc/ascend_install.info \
  -it $IMAGE bash
```

::::
::::{tab-item} Build from source

You can build all from source.

- Install `vllm-ascend`, refer to set up using python.

::::
:::::

## Deployment

### Single-card Deployment (Recommended)

Qwen3.5-0.8B is an ultra-compact model with only 1.73GB weights, which can easily fit on a single Atlas 800 A2 (64G) NPU card. The following is the recommended single-card deployment configuration.

```bash
#!/bin/sh
# Load model from ModelScope to speed up download
export VLLM_USE_MODELSCOPE=true
# To reduce memory fragmentation and avoid out of memory
export PYTORCH_NPU_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True
export TASK_QUEUE_ENABLE=1
export CPU_AFFINITY_CONF=1
export OMP_NUM_THREADS=8
# Use jemalloc for better multi-thread memory allocation
export LD_PRELOAD=/usr/lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libjemalloc.so.2

vllm serve Qwen/Qwen3.5-0.8B \
--host 0.0.0.0 \
--port 8000 \
--tensor-parallel-size 1 \
--seed 1024 \
--served-model-name qwen3.5-0.8b \
--max-num-seqs 256 \
--max-model-len 4096 \
--max-num-batched-tokens 8192 \
--trust-remote-code \
--gpu-memory-utilization 0.85 \
--compilation-config '{"mode": "none", "cudagraph_mode": "FULL_DECODE_ONLY"}'
```

### Multi-card Deployment (Higher Throughput)

For higher throughput, you can use tensor parallelism across multiple NPU cards. The following shows TP=2 (best cost/performance ratio) and TP=4 (highest throughput) configurations.

```bash
#!/bin/sh
# Load model from ModelScope to speed up download
export VLLM_USE_MODELSCOPE=true
# To reduce memory fragmentation and avoid out of memory
export PYTORCH_NPU_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True
export TASK_QUEUE_ENABLE=1
export CPU_AFFINITY_CONF=1
export OMP_NUM_THREADS=8
export HCCL_OP_EXPANSION_MODE=AIV
# Use jemalloc for better multi-thread memory allocation
export LD_PRELOAD=/usr/lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libjemalloc.so.2

# TP=2 (best cost/performance ratio) - update ASCEND_RT_VISIBLE_DEVICES to your available cards
ASCEND_RT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1 \
vllm serve Qwen/Qwen3.5-0.8B \
--host 0.0.0.0 \
--port 8000 \
--tensor-parallel-size 2 \
--seed 1024 \
--served-model-name qwen3.5-0.8b \
--max-num-seqs 256 \
--max-model-len 4096 \
--max-num-batched-tokens 8192 \
--trust-remote-code \
--gpu-memory-utilization 0.85 \
--compilation-config '{"mode": "none", "cudagraph_mode": "FULL_DECODE_ONLY"}' \
--additional-config '{"enable_async_exponential":true}'
```

__Notice:__

The parameters are explained as follows:

- `--tensor-parallel-size` 1/2/4 controls the tensor parallelism size. For Qwen3.5-0.8B, TP=1 is sufficient for most use cases; use TP=2 or TP=4 for higher throughput.
- `--max-model-len` represents the context length, which is the maximum value of the input plus output for a single request. For 0.8B models, 4096 is a practical balance between memory usage and context capacity.
- `--max-num-seqs` indicates the maximum number of requests that each DP group is allowed to process. If the number of requests sent to the service exceeds this limit, the excess requests will remain in a waiting state and will not be scheduled. Note that the time spent in the waiting state is also counted in metrics such as TTFT and TPOT. Therefore, when testing performance, it is generally recommended that `--max-num-seqs` * `--data-parallel-size` >= the actual total concurrency.
- `--max-num-batched-tokens` represents the maximum number of tokens that the model can process in a single step. Currently, vLLM v1 scheduling enables ChunkPrefill/SplitFuse by default, which means:
  - (1) If the input length of a request is greater than `--max-num-batched-tokens`, it will be divided into multiple rounds of computation according to `--max-num-batched-tokens`;
  - (2) Decode requests are prioritized for scheduling, and prefill requests are scheduled only if there is available capacity.
  - Generally, if `--max-num-batched-tokens` is set to a larger value, the overall latency will be lower, but the pressure on GPU memory (activation value usage) will be greater.
- `--gpu-memory-utilization` represents the proportion of HBM that vLLM will use for actual inference. Its essential function is to calculate the available kv_cache size. During the warm-up phase (referred to as profile run in vLLM), vLLM records the peak GPU memory usage during an inference process with an input size of `--max-num-batched-tokens`. The available kv_cache size is then calculated as: `--gpu-memory-utilization` * HBM size - peak GPU memory usage. Therefore, the larger the value of `--gpu-memory-utilization`, the more kv_cache can be used. However, since the GPU memory usage during the warm-up phase may differ from that during actual inference (e.g., due to uneven EP load), setting `--gpu-memory-utilization` too high may lead to OOM (Out of Memory) issues during actual inference. The default value is `0.9`.
- `--compilation-config` contains configurations related to the aclgraph graph mode. The most significant configurations are "cudagraph_mode" and "cudagraph_capture_sizes", which have the following meanings:
  "cudagraph_mode": represents the specific graph mode. Currently, "PIECEWISE" and "FULL_DECODE_ONLY" are supported. The graph mode is mainly used to reduce the cost of operator dispatch. Currently, "FULL_DECODE_ONLY" is recommended.
- "cudagraph_capture_sizes": represents different levels of graph modes. The default value is [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 24, 32, 40,..., `--max-num-seqs`]. In the graph mode, the input for graphs at different levels is fixed, and inputs between levels are automatically padded to the next level. Currently, the default setting is recommended. Only in some scenarios is it necessary to set this separately to achieve optimal performance.
- `--additional-config` with `enable_async_exponential: true` enables asynchronous exponential computation that overlaps with model execution, which significantly stabilizes throughput by preventing TTFT degradation over time. This is the single most effective optimization for sustained performance.

### Performance Optimization Tips

The following environment variables and configurations are critical for achieving optimal performance on Ascend NPU:

| Optimization | Configuration | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| **cudagraph FULL_DECODE_ONLY** | `--compilation-config '{"mode":"none","cudagraph_mode":"FULL_DECODE_ONLY"}'` | +243% throughput (most significant) |
| **TASK_QUEUE_ENABLE** | `export TASK_QUEUE_ENABLE=1` | +10-13% throughput |
| **CPU_AFFINITY_CONF** | `export CPU_AFFINITY_CONF=1` | +1-3% throughput |
| **HCCL_OP_EXPANSION_MODE** | `export HCCL_OP_EXPANSION_MODE=AIV` (TP>1 only) | +5-8% throughput |
| **jemalloc** | `export LD_PRELOAD=/usr/lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libjemalloc.so.2` | +3-5% throughput |
| **enable_async_exponential** | `--additional-config '{"enable_async_exponential":true}'` | Stabilizes throughput, eliminates TTFT degradation |
| **expandable_segments** | `export PYTORCH_NPU_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True` | Reduces memory fragmentation |

## Functional Verification

Once your server is started, you can query the model with input prompts:

```
curl http://localhost:8000/v1/completions \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
        "model": "qwen3.5-0.8b",
        "prompt": "The future of AI is",
        "max_completion_tokens": 50,
        "temperature": 0
    }'
```

## Accuracy Evaluation

### Using AISBench

1. Refer to Using AISBench for details.
2. After execution, you can get the result, here is the result of `Qwen3.5-0.8B` in `vllm-ascend:v0.17.0rc1` for reference only.

| dataset | version | metric | mode | qwen3.5-0.8b-vllm |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| gsm8k | 7cd45e | accuracy | gen | 55.42 |

## Performance

### Using AISBench

Refer to Using AISBench for performance evaluation for details.

### Using vLLM Benchmark

Run performance evaluation of `Qwen3.5-0.8B` as an example.

Refer to vllm benchmark for more details.

There are three `vllm bench` subcommands:

- `latency`: Benchmark the latency of a single batch of requests.
- `serve`: Benchmark the online serving throughput.
- `throughput`: Benchmark offline inference throughput.

Take the `serve` as an example. Run the code as follows.

```
export VLLM_USE_MODELSCOPE=true
vllm bench serve --model Qwen/Qwen3.5-0.8B --dataset-name random --random-input 512 --random-output 128 --num-prompts 64 --request-rate inf --save-result --result-dir ./
```

After about several minutes, you can get the performance evaluation result.

### Performance Reference Data

The following table shows the performance of Qwen3.5-0.8B on Atlas 800 A2 (Ascend 910, 64GB HBM) with full optimization applied:

| Config | Output tok/s | Req/s | Mean TTFT (ms) | Mean TPOT (ms) | Peak tok/s |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **TP1** (optimized) | 1,917 | 14.98 | 2,150 | 16.59 | 5,312 |
| **TP2** (optimized) | 2,411 | 18.83 | 1,352 | 15.98 | 5,312 |
| **TP2** (optimized + async_exp) | **~4,070** | ~31.6 | **~660** | **~10.2** | **7,040** |
| **TP4** (optimized) | 2,603 | 20.34 | 1,349 | 14.03 | 6,912 |

> Test parameters: seed=42, burstiness=1.0, 64 prompts, input=512, output=128. All results exclude cold start (3 warmup requests).

### Optimization Journey

| Date | Configuration | Throughput (tok/s) | Cumulative Improvement |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 2026-03-17 | enforce-eager, TP4 | 24 | baseline |
| 2026-03-19 | cudagraph + env vars, TP2 | 1,157 | +47x |
| 2026-03-20 | + parameter tuning, TP2 | 2,679 | +112x |
| 2026-03-27 | + async_exponential, TP2 | **4,270** | **+178x** |

zh.content:
## 简介

Qwen3.5 代表了一次重大飞跃，集成了多模态学习、架构效率、强化学习规模和全球化可访问性等方面的突破，为开发者和企业提供了前所未有的能力和效率。

Qwen3.5-0.8B 是 Qwen3.5 家族中的超紧凑模型，采用混合注意力 + Mamba（Gated DeltaNet）架构，结合全注意力和线性注意力层实现高效推理。仅有 0.8B 参数和约 1.73GB 权重大小，专为边缘部署和资源受限场景设计，同时支持多模态能力（文本 + 图像 + 视频）。

本文档将展示模型的主要验证步骤，包括支持特性、特性配置、环境准备、单节点部署、精度和性能评估。

`Qwen3.5-0.8B` 模型首次在 `vllm-ascend:v0.17.0rc1` 中支持。

## 支持特性

请参考支持特性获取模型的支持特性矩阵。

请参考特性指南获取特性配置方式。

## 环境准备

### 模型权重

- `Qwen3.5-0.8B`（BF16 版本）：需要 1 张 Atlas 800 A2（64G × 1）NPU 卡。[下载模型权重](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.5-0.8B) 或 [ModelScope](https://modelscope.cn/models/Qwen/Qwen3.5-0.8B)

建议将模型权重下载到共享目录，如 `/root/.cache/` 或 `/tmp/models/`。

### 安装

:::::{tab-set}
::::{tab-item} 使用 Docker 镜像

例如，使用镜像 `quay.io/ascend/vllm-ascend:v0.17.0rc1`（适用于 Atlas 800 A2）和 `quay.io/ascend/vllm-ascend:v0.17.0rc1-a3`（适用于 Atlas 800 A3）。

根据您的机器类型选择镜像，并在节点上启动 Docker 容器，请参考使用 Docker。

```
  # 根据 device 更新 --device 参数（Atlas A2: /dev/davinci[0-7]，Atlas A3: /dev/davinci[0-15]）。
  # 根据您的环境更新 vllm-ascend 镜像。
  # 注意：请提前将权重下载到 /root/.cache。
  # 更新 vllm-ascend 镜像
  export IMAGE=m.daocloud.io/quay.io/ascend/vllm-ascend:v0.17.0rc1
  export NAME=vllm-ascend

  # 使用定义的变量运行容器
  # 注意：Qwen3.5-0.8B 仅需 1 张 NPU 卡
  docker run --rm \
  --name $NAME \
  --net=host \
  --shm-size=1g \
  --device /dev/davinci0 \
  --device /dev/davinci_manager \
  --device /dev/devmm_svm \
  --device /dev/hisi_hdc \
  -v /usr/local/dcmi:/usr/local/dcmi \
  -v /usr/local/Ascend/driver/tools/hccn_tool:/usr/local/Ascend/driver/tools/hccn_tool \
  -v /usr/local/bin/npu-smi:/usr/local/bin/npu-smi \
  -v /usr/local/Ascend/driver/lib64/:/usr/local/Ascend/driver/lib64/ \
  -v /usr/local/Ascend/driver/version.info:/usr/local/Ascend/driver/version.info \
  -v /etc/ascend_install.info:/etc/ascend_install.info \
  -it $IMAGE bash
```

::::
::::{tab-item} 从源码构建

您可以从源码构建。

- 安装 `vllm-ascend`，请参考使用 Python 安装。

::::
:::::

## 部署

### 单卡部署（推荐）

Qwen3.5-0.8B 是一个超紧凑模型，仅有 1.73GB 权重，可以轻松部署在单张 Atlas 800 A2（64G）NPU 卡上。以下是推荐的单卡部署配置。

```bash
#!/bin/sh
# 从 ModelScope 加载模型以加速下载
export VLLM_USE_MODELSCOPE=true
# 减少内存碎片，避免 OOM
export PYTORCH_NPU_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True
export TASK_QUEUE_ENABLE=1
export CPU_AFFINITY_CONF=1
export OMP_NUM_THREADS=8
# 使用 jemalloc 优化多线程内存分配
export LD_PRELOAD=/usr/lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libjemalloc.so.2

vllm serve Qwen/Qwen3.5-0.8B \
--host 0.0.0.0 \
--port 8000 \
--tensor-parallel-size 1 \
--seed 1024 \
--served-model-name qwen3.5-0.8b \
--max-num-seqs 256 \
--max-model-len 4096 \
--max-num-batched-tokens 8192 \
--trust-remote-code \
--gpu-memory-utilization 0.85 \
--compilation-config '{"mode": "none", "cudagraph_mode": "FULL_DECODE_ONLY"}'
```

### 多卡部署（更高吞吐）

如需更高吞吐，可使用多卡张量并行。以下展示 TP=2（最佳性价比）和 TP=4（最高吞吐）配置。

```bash
#!/bin/sh
# 从 ModelScope 加载模型以加速下载
export VLLM_USE_MODELSCOPE=true
# 减少内存碎片，避免 OOM
export PYTORCH_NPU_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True
export TASK_QUEUE_ENABLE=1
export CPU_AFFINITY_CONF=1
export OMP_NUM_THREADS=8
export HCCL_OP_EXPANSION_MODE=AIV
# 使用 jemalloc 优化多线程内存分配
export LD_PRELOAD=/usr/lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libjemalloc.so.2

# TP=2（最佳性价比）- 请根据可用卡号更新 ASCEND_RT_VISIBLE_DEVICES
ASCEND_RT_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1 \
vllm serve Qwen/Qwen3.5-0.8B \
--host 0.0.0.0 \
--port 8000 \
--tensor-parallel-size 2 \
--seed 1024 \
--served-model-name qwen3.5-0.8b \
--max-num-seqs 256 \
--max-model-len 4096 \
--max-num-batched-tokens 8192 \
--trust-remote-code \
--gpu-memory-utilization 0.85 \
--compilation-config '{"mode": "none", "cudagraph_mode": "FULL_DECODE_ONLY"}' \
--additional-config '{"enable_async_exponential":true}'
```

__参数说明：__

各参数解释如下：

- `--tensor-parallel-size` 1/2/4 控制张量并行大小。对于 Qwen3.5-0.8B，TP=1 即可满足大多数使用场景；使用 TP=2 或 TP=4 可获得更高吞吐。
- `--max-model-len` 表示上下文长度，即单个请求输入加输出的最大值。对于 0.8B 模型，4096 是内存使用与上下文容量之间的实用平衡点。
- `--max-num-seqs` 表示每个 DP 组允许处理的最大请求数。如果发送到服务的请求超过此限制，超出的请求将保持等待状态，不会被调度。请注意，等待时间也会计入 TTFT 和 TPOT 等指标。因此，在测试性能时，通常建议 `--max-num-seqs` * `--data-parallel-size` >= 实际总并发数。
- `--max-num-batched-tokens` 表示模型在单步中可处理的最大 token 数。目前 vLLM v1 调度默认启用 ChunkPrefill/SplitFuse，这意味着：
  - (1) 如果请求的输入长度大于 `--max-num-batched-tokens`，将按照 `--max-num-batched-tokens` 分多轮计算；
  - (2) Decode 请求优先调度，仅在有空余容量时才调度 prefill 请求。
  - 通常，`--max-num-batched-tokens` 设置越大，整体延迟越低，但对显存（激活值使用）的压力也越大。
- `--gpu-memory-utilization` 表示 vLLM 用于实际推理的 HBM 比例。其本质功能是计算可用的 kv_cache 大小。在预热阶段（vLLM 中称为 profile run），vLLM 记录输入大小为 `--max-num-batched-tokens` 的推理过程中的峰值显存使用量。可用 kv_cache 大小计算为：`--gpu-memory-utilization` * HBM 大小 - 峰值显存使用量。因此 `--gpu-memory-utilization` 越大，可用的 kv_cache 越多。但由于预热阶段的显存使用可能与实际推理时不同（例如 EP 负载不均），设置过高可能导致实际推理时出现 OOM（内存不足）。默认值为 `0.9`。
- `--compilation-config` 包含与 aclgraph 图模式相关的配置。最重要的配置项是 "cudagraph_mode" 和 "cudagraph_capture_sizes"，含义如下：
  "cudagraph_mode"：表示具体的图模式。目前支持 "PIECEWISE" 和 "FULL_DECODE_ONLY"。图模式主要用于降低算子调度开销。目前推荐使用 "FULL_DECODE_ONLY"。
- "cudagraph_capture_sizes"：表示不同级别的图模式。默认值为 [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 24, 32, 40,..., `--max-num-seqs`]。在图模式下，不同级别图的输入是固定的，级别之间的输入会自动填充到下一个级别。目前推荐使用默认设置。仅在部分场景下需要单独设置以达到最优性能。
- `--additional-config` 中设置 `enable_async_exponential: true` 可启用异步指数计算，与模型执行重叠，通过防止 TTFT 随时间劣化来显著稳定吞吐。这是持续性能优化中最有效的一项。

### 性能优化建议

以下环境变量和配置对于在 Ascend NPU 上实现最佳性能至关重要：

| 优化项 | 配置 | 效果 |
|---|---|---|
| **cudagraph FULL_DECODE_ONLY** | `--compilation-config '{"mode":"none","cudagraph_mode":"FULL_DECODE_ONLY"}'` | +243% 吞吐（效果最显著） |
| **TASK_QUEUE_ENABLE** | `export TASK_QUEUE_ENABLE=1` | +10-13% 吞吐 |
| **CPU_AFFINITY_CONF** | `export CPU_AFFINITY_CONF=1` | +1-3% 吞吐 |
| **HCCL_OP_EXPANSION_MODE** | `export HCCL_OP_EXPANSION_MODE=AIV`（仅 TP>1） | +5-8% 吞吐 |
| **jemalloc** | `export LD_PRELOAD=/usr/lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libjemalloc.so.2` | +3-5% 吞吐 |
| **enable_async_exponential** | `--additional-config '{"enable_async_exponential":true}'` | 稳定吞吐，消除 TTFT 劣化 |
| **expandable_segments** | `export PYTORCH_NPU_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True` | 减少内存碎片 |

## 功能验证

服务启动后，您可以使用输入提示查询模型：

```
curl http://localhost:8000/v1/completions \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
        "model": "qwen3.5-0.8b",
        "prompt": "The future of AI is",
        "max_completion_tokens": 50,
        "temperature": 0
    }'
```

## 精度评估

### 使用 AISBench

1. 请参考使用 AISBench 了解详情。
2. 执行后可获取结果，以下是 `Qwen3.5-0.8B` 在 `vllm-ascend:v0.17.0rc1` 上的结果，仅供参考。

| 数据集 | 版本 | 指标 | 模式 | qwen3.5-0.8b-vllm |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| gsm8k | 7cd45e | accuracy | gen | 55.42 |

## 性能

### 使用 AISBench

请参考使用 AISBench 进行性能评估了解详情。

### 使用 vLLM Benchmark

以 `Qwen3.5-0.8B` 为例运行性能评估。

请参考 vllm benchmark 了解更多详情。

`vllm bench` 有三个子命令：

- `latency`：测试单批次请求的延迟。
- `serve`：测试在线服务吞吐。
- `throughput`：测试离线推理吞吐。

以 `serve` 为例，运行代码如下：

```
export VLLM_USE_MODELSCOPE=true
vllm bench serve --model Qwen/Qwen3.5-0.8B --dataset-name random --random-input 512 --random-output 128 --num-prompts 64 --request-rate inf --save-result --result-dir ./
```

大约几分钟后，即可获取性能评估结果。

### 性能参考数据

以下表格展示了 Qwen3.5-0.8B 在 Atlas 800 A2（Ascend 910，64GB HBM）上应用全部优化后的性能：

| 配置 | Output tok/s | Req/s | 平均 TTFT (ms) | 平均 TPOT (ms) | Peak tok/s |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **TP1**（优化后） | 1,917 | 14.98 | 2,150 | 16.59 | 5,312 |
| **TP2**（优化后） | 2,411 | 18.83 | 1,352 | 15.98 | 5,312 |
| **TP2**（优化 + async_exp） | **~4,070** | ~31.6 | **~660** | **~10.2** | **7,040** |
| **TP4**（优化后） | 2,603 | 20.34 | 1,349 | 14.03 | 6,912 |

> 测试参数：seed=42, burstiness=1.0, 64 prompts, input=512, output=128。所有结果排除冷启动（3 个预热请求）。

### 优化历程

| 日期 | 配置 | 吞吐 (tok/s) | 累计提升 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 2026-03-17 | enforce-eager, TP4 | 24 | 基线 |
| 2026-03-19 | cudagraph + 环境变量, TP2 | 1,157 | +47x |
| 2026-03-20 | + 参数调优, TP2 | 2,679 | +112x |
| 2026-03-27 | + async_exponential, TP2 | **4,270** | **+178x** |
